summary: Shakedown Street opener, soaring Eyes‑Estimated‑Crazy Fingers, strong second set
blurb: The concert launches with a fiery Shakedown Street and rolls through a tight first set. The second set shines with the classic Eyes Of The World → Estimated Prophet → Crazy Fingers → Uncle John's Band sequence plus an energetic double encore. Brent’s keyboards and vocals are front‑and‑center, especially on I Will Take You Home and the finale.
review: July 14, 1990 at Foxboro Stadium captured a summer‑night blaze that still feels fresh for Deadheads. Jerry opened with a crunchy Shakedown Street where his tone cut through the heat, even if his vocal was a touch dry on the verses. The band then settled into a groove that carried them into a seamless second‑set marathon: Eyes Of The World launched into an inspired Estimated Prophet, which morphed into a funk‑laden Crazy Fingers and rolled straight into Uncle John's Band with all the tight transitions fans crave. Brent Mydland’s raspy, echo‑laden vocals on I Will Take You Home and his frantic keyboard runs throughout the set added extra fire just weeks before his untimely passing. Phil laid down “lots of Phil” in the low end, while Mickey and Billy kept a powerful dual‑drum foundation that drove the high‑energy moments like Goin’ Down The Road Feeling Bad and Throwing Stones. The night closed with a heartfelt double encore – The Last Time fading into And We Bid You Good Night – leaving the stadium humming long after the lights went out.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (10 out of 15) are marked positive, praising the set flow, standout songs, and especially the Eyes Of The World → Estimated Prophet → Crazy Fingers sequence. Only a few mixed reviews note minor audio quirks or slower first‑set moments; no consistent negative criticism appears. This strong consensus warrants a solid +1.6 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with excellent fidelity; showcases the standout Eyes Of The World → Estimated Prophet → Crazy Fingers jam and overall high‑energy second set.

key_highlights: Fiery Shakedown Street opener with Brent’s vocal presence
Seamless Eyes Of The World → Estimated Prophet → Crazy Fingers → Uncle John's Band sequence
Brent’s keyboard frenzy on I Will Take You Home and throughout the set
Double encore featuring The Last Time / And We Bid You Good Night
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
And We Bid You Good Night

band_performance.Jerry: Tone praised and bright solo on Eyes Of The World; voice a bit dry on Shakedown but overall strong stage presence.
band_performance.Phil: Bass provides solid foundation with plenty of 'lots of Phil' moments throughout the night.
band_performance.Brent: Raspy, echo‑laden vocals and frantic keyboard runs dominate; highlighted on I Will Take You Home and during the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Powerful drumming especially in post‑drum segments of the second set adds drive to the high‑energy moments.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers tight, propulsive rhythms that lock in with Mickey, underpinning the groove from Shakedown Street through the encore.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
And We Bid You Good Night
